Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. An unsolicited guide to being a researcher [pdf] 125 upvotes, 19 comments
  2. Side-Effectful Expressions in C (2023) 22 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Can you get root with only a cigarette lighter? (2024) 156 upvotes, 31 comments
  4. The quadratic problem nobody fixed 30 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. The gold standard of optimization: A look under the hood of RollerCoaster Tycoon 535 upvotes, 145 comments
  6. I tried Karpathy's Autoresearch on an old research project 58 upvotes, 12 comments
  7. Bombadil: Property-based testing for web UIs 192 upvotes, 76 comments
  8. PC Gamer recommends RSS readers in a 37mb article that just keeps downloading 783 upvotes, 358 comments
  9. Trivy under attack again: Widespread GitHub Actions tag compromise secrets 87 upvotes, 31 comments
  10. Local Stack Archived their GitHub repo and requires an account to run 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. US govt pays TotalEnergies nearly $1B to stop US offshore wind projects 126 upvotes, 50 comments
  12. BIO: The Bao I/O Coprocessor 55 upvotes, 18 comments
  13. “Collaboration” is bullshit 192 upvotes, 92 comments
  14. GitHub appears to be struggling with measly three nines availability 374 upvotes, 194 comments
  15. Digs: Offline-first iOS app to browse your Discogs vinyl collection 18 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. The future of version control 632 upvotes, 356 comments
  17. iPhone 17 Pro Demonstrated Running a 400B LLM 312 upvotes, 185 comments
  18. Show HN: The King Wen Permutation: [52, 10, 2] 51 upvotes, 24 comments
  19. If DSPy is so great, why isn't anyone using it? 169 upvotes, 103 comments
  20. Walmart: ChatGPT checkout converted 3x worse than website 327 upvotes, 227 comments
  21. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 551 upvotes, 410 comments
  22. Migrating to the EU 718 upvotes, 570 comments
  23. Tin Can, a 'landline' for kids 286 upvotes, 231 comments
  24. Show HN: Threadprocs – executables sharing one address space (0-copy pointers) 44 upvotes, 31 comments
  25. Is it a pint? 127 upvotes, 114 comments
  26. Cyber.mil serving file downloads using TLS certificate which expired 3 days ago 120 upvotes, 110 comments
  27. I built an AI receptionist for a mechanic shop 131 upvotes, 143 comments
  28. General Motors is assisting with the restoration of a rare EV1 72 upvotes, 82 comments
  29. Cyberattack on vehicle breathalyzer company leaves drivers stranded in the US 117 upvotes, 153 comments
  30. Two pilots dead after plane and ground vehicle collide at LaGuardia 175 upvotes, 303 comments

Last generated 3:30am, 24 March 2026. Source here.
